flashcards.front: Define the multiplicative group of integers modulo p (Zp*)
flashcards.back: The set {1, 2, 3, ..., p-1} with multiplication modulo p, where p is a prime number. It forms an abelian group of order p-1.

flashcards.front: What is the order of the multiplicative group Zp*?
flashcards.back: The order of Zp* is p-1, since it contains all integers from 1 to p-1 that are relatively prime to p.

flashcards.front: Define a generator (primitive root) in Zp*
flashcards.back: An element g in Zp* is a generator if its powers generate all elements of the group, i.e., {g¹, g², ..., g^(p-1)} = Zp*.
For any integer a coprime to n, a^φ(n) ≡ 1 (mod n), where φ(n) is Euler's totient function counting integers ≤ n coprime to n.
If p is prime and p ∤ a, then a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p). This is a special case of Euler's Theorem where n = p is prime.
A permutation is a bijective function from a set {1, 2, ..., n} to itself. It rearranges the elements of the set.

flashcards.front: What is the symmetric group Sn?
flashcards.back: The set of all permutations of n symbols, denoted Sn, with n! elements. It forms a non-abelian group under composition.
A cycle (a1 a2 a3 ...) represents mapping a1→a2, a2→a3, ..., last→a1. Disjoint cycles commute.

flashcards.front: How do you find the order of a permutation?
flashcards.back: Express permutation as product of disjoint cycles. The order is the LCM (Least Common Multiple) of the lengths of all cycles.
A permutation is even if it can be expressed as product of even number of transpositions; odd if product of odd number of transpositions.

flashcards.front: What is the alternating group An?
flashcards.back: The subgroup of Sn containing all even permutations. It has n!/2 elements and is normal in Sn.
Every finite group is isomorphic to a subgroup of the symmetric group Sn for some n (the order of the group).
A transposition is a 2-cycle (i j) that swaps exactly two elements i and j, leaving others fixed. It is always an odd permutation.

flashcards.front: Define the sign (signature) of a permutation
flashcards.back: The sign of a permutation is +1 for even permutations and -1 for odd permutations. It equals (-1)^k where k is the number of transpositions.

flashcards.front: What is a cycle decomposition of a permutation?
flashcards.back: Writing a permutation as a product of disjoint cycles. Every permutation can be uniquely expressed (up to order) as disjoint cycles.
